turns.speaker: user
turns.text: please discuss for me the Polish Enterprise Fund VIII, stuff like their investing strategy, assets, investments, portfolio et.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
The Polish Enterprise Fund VIII (PEF VIII) is a private equity fund managed by Enterprise Investors (EI), one of the largest and most experienced private equity firms in Central and Eastern Europe. Established in 1990, Enterprise Investors boasts a 30-year track record of investments in the region, and the Polish Enterprise Fund VIII is their ninth fund. PEF VIII primarily focuses on investments in Poland, as well as in the nearby Central and Eastern European countries.

Investing Strategy:
PEF VIII's investing strategy revolves around leveraging the experience and expertise of the Enterprise Investors team to identify and invest in opportunities across various sectors. The fund primarily focuses on making growth equity investments, buyouts, and expansion financing in mid-market companies with significant growth potential, which can be achieved with the help of seasoned management teams and dedicated operational improvements.

Some of the key sectors that the fund targets include consumer goods and retail, healthcare, financial services, and the IT sector. The fund places a particular emphasis on companies that are export-oriented, have strong intellectual property, and demonstrate potential for regional or international growth.

Assets:
PEF VIII was launched in 2016 and has a target size of €500 million, with commitments from a well-diversified group of limited partners, primarily from Western countries. The fund focuses on acquiring majority or substantial minority stakes in companies, with investments typically ranging from €20 million to €75 million per deal.

Investments:
Some of the notable investments made by PEF VIII include:

1. Anwim - A leading fuel retailer in Poland, operating under the Moje MAZ brand. PEF VIII acquired a 65% stake in Anwim in 2018 and aims to support the company's expansion and growth plans through the development of new retail spaces, gas stations, and other services.

2. Nu-Med Group - A network of oncology and cardiology-focused diagnostic imaging and treatment centers in Poland. PEF VIII acquired a majority stake in Nu-Med Group in 2017 to propel the growth of the innovative healthcare provider by increasing the range and quality of services offered to patients.

3. Velvet Care - A leading manufacturer of tissue paper products for households and professional users in Poland. PEF VIII acquired 100% ownership in Velvet Care in 2018, with the investment aimed at helping the company expand both domestically and internationally while modernizing its manufacturing capabilities.

Portfolio:
The PEF VIII portfolio comprises companies operating in diverse sectors like automotive components, healthcare, retail, and IT services. The portfolio showcases the fund's focus on capitalizing on market trends and investing in businesses with strong growth potential, excellent management teams, and innovative products or services.

In summary, the Polish Enterprise Fund VIII is a dynamic private equity fund that seeks to invest in and grow businesses with strong potential for success in Central and Eastern Europe. By adopting a strategic approach to investments, the fund aims to create value and returns for its investors, while contributing to the regional economic growth and development.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: jakie inwestycje wykonał Enterprise investors i wykresik w latach

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Enterprise Investors (EI) to jedna z największych i najbardziej doświadczonych firm private equity w Europie Środkowo-Wschodniej. Zarządzają one 10 funduszami inwestycyjnymi. Poniżej przedstawiam kilka przykładowych inwestycji dokonanych przez EI w ostatnich latach:

1. Anwim (2018) - Operująca pod marką Moje MAZ, jedna z czołowych firm detalicznej sprzedaży paliw w Polsce. EI kupili 65% udziałów firmy Anwim.
2. Nu-Med Group (2017) - Sieć centrów diagnostyki obrazowej oraz leczenia onkologicznego i kardiologicznego w Polsce. EI nabyło większościowy udział w Grupie Nu-Med.
3. Velvet Care (2018) - Wiodący producent papieru toaletowego i innych produktów papierowych dla odbiorców biznesowych i domowych w Polsce. EI przejęli 100% udziałów w Velvet Care.
4. Śnieżka (2018) - Wiodący producent farb, emalii i lakierów w Polsce. EI sprzedał swoje udziały na rzecz austriackiej firmy – Greiner Holding.
5. Opteamum Krajowe Centrum Optyki (2016) - Prowadząca sieć optyczną w Polsce. Enterprise Investors zakupiła udziały mniejszościowe w tej firmie.
6. EEC Group (2021) - Wykonawca interaktywnego montażu fotowoltaicznego w Polsce. EI wykupił 80% własności w EEC Group.

Niestety, jako model języka AI, nie mogę stworzyć i przedstawić wykresów na bieżąco, jednak możesz znaleźć wiele przydatnych informacji na stronie internetowej Enterprise Investors (https://www.ei.com.pl/en/home-page/) oraz w ich raportach rocznych.
